description: You are acting as a technical-writer who has been asked to perform a very quick security-phrase scan on the project documentation that sits in /home/user/docs. 1. Scan **all .md files directly inside /home/user/docs** (do NOT descend into sub-directories). • Treat the search as **case-insensitive**. • Look only for the three exact keyword fragments: password, secret and api_key. 2. For every file that contains at least one hit, write the results to a new log file called /home/user/docs/security-scan.log in the **exact** format shown below. 3. Finish the log with a “Summary” block that shows the **total files scanned** and the **total number of individual hits** found. 4. The whole task should take no more than three or four standard shell commands (for example a single grep pipeline and one or two supporting commands). LOG-FILE FORMAT (must match character-for-character; no extra spaces or blank lines): –––––––––––– FILE: <relative-file-name.md> <line-number>:<full line text exactly as it appears in the file> …repeat line-number lines as needed… --FILE END-- …repeat the above FILE block as needed… Summary: Total files scanned: <number> Total hits: <number> –––––––––––– • “<relative-file-name.md>” is the file name without any leading path (e.g., setup.md). • “<line-number>” starts at 1 for the first line in each file. Once the commands have been executed successfully, the sole deliverable is the correctly formatted file /home/user/docs/security-scan.log. Automated tests will open that file to verify: a) that every hit line is present; b) that the FILE blocks appear in lexicographic order of the file names; c) that the Summary block values are correct; d) that there are no superfluous blank lines or spaces anywhere in the log. You do not need to display the log on screen—just ensure the file exists with exactly the required content.

4: "--FILE END--"

8: "Summary:"  
9: "Total files scanned: 3"  
10: "Total hits: 3"

(No extra lines before, after, or between the lines above.)

The agent will be graded against this precise file content.
